title: My work on the news
text:
1. The South American Microbiome Archive


CNN Chile interviewed me and other microbiome experts about the project. The interview is part of the section ‘Futuro 360’ and can be found online in YouTube (from minute 00:48 to 08:50).









El Mercurio, the largest newspaper in Chile, covered my research on the gut microbiome of South American populations.







APC Microbiome Ireland featured saMBA on the section ‘Scientific Excellence’ along with a quote of my reflection on my career path (pages 4-5)







Microbiome Digest featured our work on its daily news.
The Microbiologist covered the relevance of our work in an opinion piece writen by Yang Yue.
University College Cork Research News featured our work and highlighted its relevance.




2. Open Science: Transform your house into a laboratory


A university news outlet covered the release of our science communication book. The book has instructions to perform simple scientific experiments and their explanations. It was released for free during the COVID-19 lockdown to encourage kids to learn about science. The book is free online (Spanish only).

title: Benjamin Valderrama
text:
Short Bio
I’m a Computational Biologist 💻🧬 with seven years of research experience distilling key biological insight from large and complex multidimensional datasets. I’ve held positions in Academia, Industry and their interface. I’ve also worked as a consultant and analyst for academic research groups.
I’m currently doing my PhD, where I focus on the balanced integration of global-scale microbiome datasets with biologically-grounded analytical approaches to unravel the complexity of the microbiome-gut-brain axis. My work is supervised by Professors John F. Cryan and Gerard Clarke at the APC microbiome Ireland.
In my daily life I like reading books (mostly non-fiction), hiking, drinking coffee and doing 3D design and printing.


Areas of research


Large-scale analyses of the microbiome-gut-brain axis across global populations.
Microbiome’s modulation of gut barrier integrity and its effects on gut-brain health.
Development of Probiotics and Psychobiotics.

Complete list of publications
A full list of all my publications can be found at Google Scholar and ORCID.
 


Selected publications in key areas

Court, bioRxiv.





2. Probiotics and Psychobiotics

From in silico screening to in vivo validation in zebrafish - a framework for reeling in the right psychobiotics, (2024), Benjamin Valderrama, Isabelle Daly, Eoin Gunnigle, Kenneth J. O’Riordan, Maciej Chichlowski, Sagarika Banerjee, Alicja A. Skowronski, Neeraj Pandey, John F. Cryan, Gerard Clarke, Jatin Nagpal, Foods & Function.





3. Microbiome data analysis


Orchestrating Microbiome Analysis with Bioconductor, (2025), Tuomas Borman, Giulio Benedetti, Geraldson Muluh, Aura Raulo, Benjamin Valderrama, Artur Sannikov, Stefanie Peschel, Yihan Liu, Rasmus Hindstrom, OMA consortium, Katariina Parnanen, Christian L. Muller, Aki S. Havulinna, Sudarshan Shetty, Marcel Ramos, Domenick J. Braccia, Hector Corrada Bravo, Felix M. Ernst, Levi Waldron, Thomaz F. S. Bastiaanssen, Himel Mallick, Leo Lahti. Under review at Nature Biotechnology.
Chapter 26 : Machine Learning - Orchestrating Microbiome Analysis (OMA) book, (2025), Benjamin Valderrama. The review of the chapter is open to anyone, and can be accessed here.


 



My Global Co-authorship Map

title: The South American MicroBiome Archive (saMBA)
text:
The South American MicroBiome Archive (saMBA)
The South American Microbiome Archive (saMBA) is the largest archive of gut microbiome samples from South American populations analysed under an unified workflow.
The article describes the process of gathering and analysing the microbiome data, as well as the main findings. The harmonised dataset can be downloaded from Zenodo and the workflow used in the analysis is available on GitHub. All generated resources, including the article, are publicly available to propel microbiome research globally, specially in other neglected world regions.
The motivation to start this project is described here. If it resonated and you’re interested in collaborating, please get in touch.


Plans for future developments
Below I share some ideas to expand saMBA. I made this public to keep the users informed and to invite people interested in contributing in any of these points to reach out.


Build an R package to facilitate the download of the outputs in Zenodo
Implement the workflow as a Docker image so users can deploy it.

Update (01/Jun/2025): Docker image here

Expand saMBA (the resource) to include samples from other bodysites of southamericans.
Expand the workflow to include the analysis of projects using whole genome sequencing.

title: The Middle Author Bioinformatician
text: I write about Bioinformatics, Gut Microbiome, The Microbiome-Gut-Brain axis and Science in general. I also share some personal thoughts and keep track of my path to becoming something more than just another middle author bioinformatician.
